dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7106 IH: Enhancing Skilled Nursing Facilities Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Kiggans of Virginia (for herself and Mrs. Dingell) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To amend titles XVIII and XIX of the Social Security Act to streamline care delivery in skilled nursing facilities and nursing facilities under the Medicare and Medicaid programs.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Enhancing Skilled Nursing Facilities Act.

section.header: Streamlining care delivery in skilled nursing facilities and nursing facilities

paragraph.header: Certification of post-hospital extended care services
paragraph.text: Section 1814(a)(2) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395f(a)(2)) is amended by striking who does not and all that follows through physician,, and inserting working in accordance with State law,.

paragraph.header: Supervision requirement in skilled nursing facility services
paragraph.text: Section 1819(b)(6)(A) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395i–3(b)(6)(A)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in the header, by striking Physician supervision and inserting Supervision; and
by inserting or a nurse practitioner or physician assistant, in accordance with State law after physician.

paragraph.text: Section 1842(b)(2)(C) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395u(b)(2)(C)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by inserting , a nurse practitioner, or a physician assistant after a physician; and
by striking and includes a physician assistant acting under the supervision of the physician or a nurse practitioner working in collaboration with that physician, or both.

paragraph.header: Provision of medical and other health services
paragraph.text: Section 1861(s)(2)(K)(ii) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395x(s)(2)(K)(ii)) is amended by striking or clinical nurse specialist (as defined in subsection (aa)(5)) working in collaboration (as defined in subsection (aa)(6)) with a physician (as defined in subsection (r)(1)) and inserting (as defined in subsection (aa)(5)(A)), or by a clinical nurse specialist (as defined in subsection (aa)(5)(B)) working in collaboration with a physician (as defined in subsection (r)(1)),.

Section 1819(b)(2)(B) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395i–3(b)(2)(B)) is amended by inserting (or a nurse practitioner, physician assistant, or clinical nurse specialist working in accordance with State law) after attending physician.
Section 1819(c) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395i–3(c)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (1)(A)(i), by inserting nurse practitioner, physician assistant, or clinical nurse specialist working in accordance with State law after attending physician;
in paragraph (2)(A), in the matter following clause (vi)—

clause.text: by inserting nurse practitioner, physician assistant, or clinical nurse specialist working in accordance with State law after resident’s physician; and
by inserting , nurse practitioner, physician assistant, or clinical nurse specialist working in accordance with State law after a physician; and

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (3)(A), by inserting , nurse practitioner, physician assistant, or clinical nurse specialist working in accordance with State law after physician.

paragraph.header: Certification of skilled nursing facility services and intermediate care facility services
paragraph.text: Section 1902(a)(44) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396a(a)(44)) is amended—

clause.text: by striking (or, in the case of skilled nursing facility services or intermediate care facility services and inserting (or, in the case of intermediate care facility services;

by striking physician) certifies and inserting physician, or, in the case of skilled nursing facility services, a physician, or a nurse practitioner, a physician assistant, or a clinical nurse specialist working in accordance with State law) certifies;
by striking a physician, or, in the case of skilled nursing facility services or and inserting a physician, or, in the case of; and

by inserting or, in the case of skilled nursing facility services, a physician, or a nurse practitioner, a physician assistant, or a clinical nurse specialist working in accordance with State law, before recertifies; and
by striking skilled nursing facility services or; and

by inserting , or, in the case of skilled nursing facility services, a physician, or a nurse practitioner, a physician assistant, or a clinical nurse specialist working in accordance with State law before the semicolon.

paragraph.header: Nursing facility services supervision and clinical records
paragraph.text: Section 1919(b)(6)(A) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396r(b)(6)(A)) is amended to read as follows:

subparagraph.text: require that the health care of every resident be provided under the supervision of a physician or nurse practitioner or a physician assistant (or, at the option of a State, under the supervision of a clinical nurse specialist);
